items.rationale: Sees ZK as a critical, rapidly accelerating field — built/published an a16z ZK canon, funds ZK grants, runs setup ceremonies, and tests zkEVMs personally.

citations.quote: research has accelerated radically as a result of web3
I'm excited to share our new zero knowledge cryptography canon.
Probably the most accessible and precise walkthrough of VDFs I've seen. They're a critical tool in ETH 2

items.rationale: Believes on-chain games will fill the niche of beloved engineering games; deeply involved with a16z games team and Lattice/OPCraft, Dark Forest.

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.
Collaborating with the games team is one of the most surreal and enjoyable parts of my week.

items.rationale: Wants public/private keypairs to replace username/password auth and give users control of data; actively backs Spruce.

citations.quote: With DIDs, we can use the public keys generated by crypto wallets... replacing username/password authentication and giving people complete control of their data.
I'm proud to be working with @wycdd and @obstropolos to make identity more private, secure, and programmable.

items.rationale: Argues centralized social platforms rug builders; sees neutral, non-unilaterally-controlled social platforms as what crypto enables.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
We need a social platform that is not unilaterally controlled... autonomous software. Crypto is the only way.

items.rationale: Excited that NFTs can hold complex mutable state and interactivity beyond static JPEGs (on-chain chess, REPLICATOR).
items.age_note: Most detailed articulation is March 2022; not restated late-2022 but consistent with his views.

citations.quote: NFTs are programmable... they can contain complex, mutable state to enable interactivity with people, programs, and other NFTs.

items.rationale: Views tokens as a powerful tool for growth, stakeholder alignment, risk distribution and decentralization when embedded in well-designed protocols.

citations.quote: Tokens are an incredible tool for growing a protocol, aligning stakeholders, distributing risk/reward, and preserving decentralization.
If tokens are the native asset class of information networks, they should provide the broad range of rights and powers that serve networks best.

items.rationale: Sees L2s as the path to scale Ethereum without compromising security; reports sequencer performing well and capacity easily expandable.

citations.quote: Layer 2 technologies, such as optimistic and zk rollups, aim to lower costs and scale layer 1 without compromising security, and are becoming more popular.
The sequencer did really well. And expanding RPC capacity should be relatively simple.

items.rationale: Frames crypto as how machines own and AI as how machines think, with the combination enabling self-directed autonomous applications — an early, under-explored space.
items.age_note: Emerging thesis articulated mid-Dec 2022; partly forward-looking.

citations.quote: AI = how machines can think / crypto = how machines can own / AI + crypto = how machines can think on their own
We only have 14 years of thought... around sovereign programs / autonomous applications.

items.rationale: Calls MEV an endlessly fascinating engineering topic and hires for it; sees it as core protocol-design surface.
items.age_note: Stated Jan 2022, not restated since; consistent with ongoing interest.

citations.quote: We connected through discussing his work on MEV — an endlessly fascinating topic for engineers.

items.rationale: Personally enthusiastic about image/text generation tools; rates Midjourney's style above DALL-E and explores prompt engineering. Non-crypto personal interest.

citations.quote: Midjourney's sense of style and creativity puts DALL-E to shame.
the effects of low cost cultural production (like DALL-E 2, Midjourney, GPT-3)

items.rationale: Post-FTX, harshly critical of CEX transparency theater — Binance's proof-of-reserves lacks Merkle tree, liability treatment, and address control evidence.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.
Negative balances are a known fatal flaw of the "proof of reserves" method Binance shared

items.rationale: Sees Apple's 30% fee and native-app restrictions as crippling crypto's mobile UX and broadly suppressing innovation.

citations.quote: Apple will cripple you, block you, or take your lunch if you try to offer a native experience.
The effects of Apple's 30% fee on innovation are radically underestimated

items.rationale: Warns that building on centralized social/platforms gets builders cut off, replaced, and demonetized — 'rugged.'

citations.quote: it isn't worth it on centralized platforms — you'll get cut off, replaced by them, blocked from monetizing. Rugged.

items.rationale: Dismisses tokenomics consultants and pie-chart allocations as nonsense; a token is a tool, not a goal, and allocation is ~5% of real protocol work.

citations.quote: "Tokenomics experts" who simply "work" with you to determine "token allocations" are a complete waste of time.
A protocol needs a clear goal. Launching a token isn't a goal.

items.rationale: Frustrated that trusted news sources and Google search are inaccurate/misleading on crypto, deterring reasonable newcomers.

citations.quote: Google search results for crypto are bad enough to deter any reasonable person. It's completely unacceptable.
Imagine explaining to a normal person how outrageous this coverage has been.

items.rationale: Regards Ethereum as the clear L1 leader by developer mindshare; celebrated a smooth Merge to PoS.

citations.quote: @ethereum is the clear leader. Its overwhelming mindshare among web3 devs
Congratulations and well done to the ETH 2 researchers, client teams, core devs

items.rationale: Highlights these as the risk tokens that underwrite their protocols — an under-appreciated, design-rich role beyond mere 'governance'; admires Maker's transparency.
items.age_note: Maker transparency praise dates to July 2021; risk-token framing restated July 2022.

citations.quote: the role of MKR and AAVE/COMP as the risk token that underwrites the protocol often seems to take a back seat rhetorically to "governance"
To asses Maker's finances, it takes one click.

risk_regime.rationale: An engineer/builder with structural long-term optimism: views crypto as ~1995-early, with tech advancing even through 'crypto winter' and consistent long-term growth despite volatility. He makes no tactical price calls but is unwaveringly bullish on building, while sharply distrustful of centralized intermediaries (CEXs) in the FTX aftermath.

notes: Eddy's view is that of a technical builder, not a trader — excitement is about protocol/cryptography primitives (ZK, on-chain games, DIDs, decentralized social, L2s, programmable NFTs) rather than asset prices. His most recent (Nov–Dec 2022, post-FTX) statements sharpen two concerns: centralized-exchange opacity/proof-of-reserves theater, and platform gatekeepers (Apple) plus media/search misinformation. A consistent late-2022 thread is contempt for token-first 'tokenomics' work and conviction that real value is in protocol design. The AI+crypto/autonomous-programs thesis is a fresh mid-Dec 2022 emergence. No contradictions requiring reversal were found; older items (Maker 2021, MEV Jan 2022) are consistent with and reinforced by later statements. Generative-AI enthusiasm is genuine but a personal/tooling interest rather than an investment sector.
